1989_12_27-INDIA-Mumbai-Iskcon_Chowpatty-Material_Enjoyment_Danger_At_Every_Step-SB_7_13_24-st2-vr01

Meta Data v8

Event Name/Title

Material Enjoyment Danger At Every Step

Event Date

27 Dec 1989

Event Location

India, Mumbai, Iskcon Chowpatty

Event Type

Temple - SB class

Transcriber

Parikshit das

Completed

21 May 2021

Source Audio

https://www.radhanathswamimedia.com/Lec/1989/89-084-Material%20Enjoyment%20Danger%20At%20Every%20Step%20(Mumbai)-SB_7_13_24-dt_89_12_27.mp3

Source Video

Verse

SB 07.13.24

Tags – Places

Material world, Vaikuntha, America, California

Tags – Pastimes

Varahdev killing Hiranyaksa, Narsinghadev killing Hiranyakasipu

Tags – Persons

Avadhut Brahman, Hiranyakasipu, Narsinghadev, Hiranyaksa, Varahadev

Tags – Themes

Hard struggle in material world, Material world – danger at every step, Doomsday philosophy or realistic view of life, Maya simply plays with us, Instead of struggling for sense enjoyment struggle for Krishna

tṛṣṇayā bhava-vāhinyā
yogyaiḥ kāmair apūryayā
karmāṇi kāryamāṇo ’haṁ
nānā-yoniṣu yojitaḥ

[SB 07.13.24]

[Because of insatiable material desires, I was being carried away by the waves of material nature’s laws, and thus I was engaging in different activities, struggling for existence in various forms of life.]

Purport

As long as a living entity wants to fulfill various types of material desire, he must continuously change from one body to accept another. Śrīla Viśvanātha Cakravartī Ṭhākura explains that as a small piece of grass falls in a river and is tossed about with different types of wood and tree branches, the living entity floats in the ocean of material existence and is dashed and tossed amidst material conditions. This is called the struggle for existence. One kind of fruitive activity causes the living being to take one form of body, and because of actions performed in that body, another body is created. One must therefore stop these material activities, and the chance to do so is given in the human form of life. Specifically, our energy to act should be engaged in the service of the Lord, for then materialistic activities will automatically stop. One must fulfill one’s desires by surrendering unto the Supreme Lord, for He knows how to fulfill them. Even though one may have material desires, one should therefore engage in the devotional service of the Lord. That will purify one’s struggle for existence.

akāmaḥ sarva-kāmo vā
mokṣa-kāma udāra-dhīḥ
tīvreṇa bhakti-yogena
yajeta puruṣaṁ param

[SB 02.03.10]

[A person who has broader intelligence, whether he be full of all material desire, without any material desire, or desiring liberation, must by all means worship the supreme whole, the Personality of Godhead.]

anyābhilāṣitā-śūnyaṁ
jṣāna-karmādy-anāvṛtam
ānukūlyena kṛṣṇānu-
śīlanaṁ bhaktir uttamā

[Bhakti-rasāmṛta-sindhu 1.1.11]

[One should render transcendental loving service to the Supreme Lord Kṛṣṇa favorably and without desire for material profit or gain through fruitive activities or philosophical speculation. That is called pure devotional service.]

Lecture Transcription

‘Because of insatiable material desires, I was being carried away by the waves of material nature’s laws, and thus I was engaging in different activities, struggling for existence in various forms of life.’

Struggle in this material world

The material existence is sometimes compared to a river as described here by Srila Vishvanath Chakravarti Thakur and the living entity is like an insignificant piece of grass that somehow or other falls into this great river and the waves of material nature's laws are carrying the insignificant jivatma from one condition of life to another. It carries us from one species of life to another, from one nationality to another, to one social setting or family to another; all temporary conditions and as described here by the Avadhut Brahman, but due to insatiable material desires, one becomes bewildered by the temporary occurrences of this world and one becomes very much attached to his condition of life. This attachment to our temporary condition of material life is based on illusion and it is the cause of our bondage and suffering in this material world. janasya moho ’yam ahaṁ mameti [SB 05.05.08]. It is described here that the conditioned soul illusioned by this false concept, that I am this body and those things in relationship to this body are mine - they constantly have to struggle hard in this material existence.

Last night someone was asking that how is it possible to remain steady in one's devotional activities, because it is so very difficult living in this world trying to be Krishna conscious. Sometimes, even though my desire is to be Krishna conscious, because it is so difficult, I naturally give up. But this material world is a place of hard struggle for existence. Whatever path of life you decide to take, it is a hard struggle. It is designed that way. It is not that the materialists they have a very easy life and the devotees are struggling very hard. Just to eat and to maintain once status quo is a tremendous endeavour, which entails tremendous sacrifice and despite all our endeavours, it is not an easy thing, to succumb to old age, to disease, to death and then again to suffer the turmoils of another birth. In the material…spiritual world there is no difficulty. In fact, there is no such thing as difficulty. It is said that in Vrindavan all talking is singing, all walking is dancing and one's constant companion is the beautiful melodious flute of Lord Hari. But in the material world, ha! wherever we go, we find strife, we find crying. How much suffering is in this world! Over half the popular…population of this world is practically in slavery being persecuted and oppressed by demoniac governments. And those who are not in that condition, they are constantly oppressed by endless competitive challenges in life, where whether they are successful or whether they are failures, they are frustrated at one time or another. It is a place where everything is uncertain.

Material world - danger at every step

Sometimes it is said the further west you go, the more the spirit of sense enjoyment is awakened. Of course, America is famous as a supreme place on earth for sense enjoyment and in America everyone in the East Coast knows that the biggest and greatest sense enjoyers are in the West Coast. And the furthermost furthest most West point in America is California. And everyone in the United States and everyone who knows in the world will admit that there is no greater place of sense enjoyment than California, where there is nice, beautiful sandy beach and everyone, their bodies are very beautiful and very strong and very healthy and the weather is always just nice and there is tremendous wealth, luxury, fresh fruit, fresh foods of all varieties. Ha! and perhaps the most famous sense enjoyers in the world, they are all living there. All the movie actresses, movie actors, all the musicians, television stars, in this way ha! people leave from all parts of America, to go to California for sense gratification. Correct, Ha! So, here we have the ideal utopian place in this world for sense gratification. And yet, they know, those persons who are there and they are living in constant fear, that any second there will be an earthquake, and their whole civilization will fall into the ocean and they will all be destroyed. It is not a question of whether it will happen, it is a question simply of on which date? Every geologist on earth admits that it must happen. And when it does happen, it will be total devastation. It was due hundreds of years ago. And the longer it does not happen, the more severe it will be when it happens, because it's getting deeper and deeper and deeper, this crack in the earth. So, here we are, people of India, those who are materialistically inclined, are very anxious to go to America, in here the topmost place of sense enjoyment in America. And yet people are living in constant fear, always looking behind them while they are enjoying their sinful activities, waiting when will that day be when everything is taken away and merged into the Pacific Ocean. So of course, this is the factual state of current events in this world. But it is also most symbolic. Everything Krishna does in this world has very deep symbolism. For one who simply wants to see and understand the message Krishna is trying to convey, is it not a fantastic message that Krishna is revealing to the world through this current condition? That no matter how much you struggle and strive and tax yourself to be successful in the process of sense gratification, … this material world you are always on a tottering platform, where at any second at any given date, everything will collapse. And you will again fall helplessly into the deep vast ocean of material suffering where despite all your successful endeavours to the glories of this world, at any second you will be devoured by the vast ocean of death and destruction. Therefore, sane man according to Bhagavad Gita, he is not attached to any of the things of this world. He is not attached for his own sense enjoyment to his prestige, his wealth, his family, even to his own body or senses. He is only attached to the service of Lord Hari and therefore, he will very very conscientiously utilize all the things of this world, in the service of the Supreme Personality of Godhead, knowing that devotional service is the means of crossing over this ocean of material existence. It is said in Srimad-Bhagavatam

samāśritā ye pada-pallava-plavaṁ
mahat-padaṁ puṇya-yaśo murāreḥ
bhavāmbudhir vatsa-padaṁ paraṁ padaṁ
padaṁ padaṁ yad vipadāṁ na teṣām

[SB 10.14.58]

[For those who have accepted the boat of the lotus feet of the Lord, who is the shelter of the cosmic manifestation and is famous as Murāri, the enemy of the Mura demon, the ocean of the material world is like the water contained in a calf’s hoof-print. Their goal is paraṁ padam, Vaikuṇṭha, the place where there are no material miseries, not the place where there is danger at every step.]

That a wise man, he never trusts or puts his faith in the pleasures of this material world. Because he knows that in this world there is danger at every step, padaṁ padaṁ yad vipadāṁ na teṣām  [SB 10.14.58]; just, we are so unconscious. We are in such ignorance of the situation around us in this world.

A devotee of the Lord, when he walks down the streets, especially in a city like Bombay, he sees that everyone, they are simply in a daze, they are simply blinded, they do not understand what is around them. Just like sometimes, you see that there is a little bird. And the bird is just dancing around here and there looking for little insects to eat. And he appears to be just very happy, enjoying his senses. And then you see, there is a large cat that is simply watching him at every step. Just waiting for the just the right moment to just leap upon him and tear him to pieces and eat him. Ha! so, you see the cat and you see the bird. The bird is about to be devoured at any minute; he will be devoured at any minute. But because he does not see the cat, why does he not see the cat? The cat is right in front of him. Because he is so busy looking for his little insects to eat or looking to have little seeds to eat. He is so engrossed in just eating his little seeds, that he doesn't see the inevitable destruction that is about to devour him. He doesn't see the temporariness, the fallibility, the vulnerability of his state of existence. Therefore, we say this bird is in complete ignorance. But this is the condition of everyone, walking around in a daze. Everyone is so engrossed, in just searching for their little seeds of material enjoyment, that they do not see that there are innumerable dangers about to devour them in any second. They do not see that death is upon them, just waiting for the right second, to tear them to pieces, to devour them. They do not see that the three-fold miseries of material existence are all around them. They do not see that with every step that they take there are innumerable miseries and dangers that can come into their life. The great sages they are aware that this material world is a place of danger at every step. This is the awareness, the consciousness of the great souls. How many steps do we take in a day? And with each step how many times are we aware of the dangers that are around us? We just walk like complete fools, we just walk, step after step after step, without even thinking of reality, that the reality of this material existence is with every step your life is in danger. And that is a fact. See the media, we can read the newspapers, we turn on the television, any news that has to do with this world, it's ninety-nine percent bad news. Time and time and time again, all we read about is how there is danger at every step. At the least expected moment your house will burn, your automobile will crash, disease will enter into your body, economically there can be disaster; there will be disaster.

Doomsday philosophy or realistic view of life?

People say why do you speak like this? This is just Doomsday philosophy. Ha, it is Doomsday philosophy. Because in this material world every moment is Doomsday, because you are one day closer…every second you are one second closer to death. Ha! with the rising and setting of the sun you are one day closer to death. Every day is bringing you closer to Doomsday. It is not our philosophy; it is the philosophy of this material world. So, if we want to be realistic about life, the only way we can have positive thinking is to make our goal Vaikuntha. Otherwise, our positive thinking in this world, this material world, Mrityuloka the place of death, it is all simply a grand illusion. We are enjoying, we are enjoying like the Californians. But at any second, the quake will come and we will all just massively fall into the ocean of death. And we do not know when it will come. All we could do is hope that it will not come too soon. That's the most we could do with all science, with all technology, with all their bombs. Just see, they can spend billions of dollars a day building bombs to destroy. With all their money can they fill that crack and stop the earthquake from happening? They cannot even endeavour to do so. Ha! there is not even a hope of preventing it from happening. The wealthiest country in the world, the wealthiest section of the country. California is the wealthiest state in all of America. With all their wealth and with all their science and with all their technology, they cannot even hope to prevent this great disaster. That is the power of this material existence. We are all completely helpless who ever we are. Therefore, an intelligent man, he knows that this…the waves of this material nature are indefatigable, undefeatable. Therefore, he gives up competing against the miseries of material existence.

Maya simply plays with us

Our whole life, we are simply competing against the miseries of existence. Why does a man work so hard throughout the day? He may think out of illusion that I am doing it out of love for my family. I am doing it out of love for my society. But actually, he is doing it in competition against the miseries of material existence; the misery of hunger, the misery of too much heat, of too much cold, the misery of dishonour. He is working simply to try to combat against these things. If I have money, then I can defeat my opponent of hunger and thirst and I will have shelter to protect me from heat and cold and I will have enough money to prevent to…to…to combat against disease, I will have medical insurance and make so many arrangements. Try to understand, this is a hard struggle for existence where we are constantly competing the infinite opponents of the sufferings and miseries of material existence. We are very small and insignificant and our opponent is almighty, as God is almighty, because mayadhyaksena prakrtih suyate sa-caracaram [Bg. 9.10], we are fighting against the divine energy of God. We cannot win. Krishna says in Bhagavad-gita that I am death personified.

Just like Hiranyakasipu, when he was fighting against Lord Narsinghadev, Lord Narsinghadev, just to fulfill Hiranyakasipu’s desire to think that he was very strong and very conquering, He allowed Hiranyakasipu to actually think he was winning the fight for some time. At one time also Lord Varahadev, when he was fighting Hiranyaksa, same principle, the Lord always lets the demons get…have a little bit of illusion that they are winning. When Varahadev was fighting against Hiranyaksa, Hiranyaksa took his mighty golden club and he leaped in the air with all his power and he roared like a lion towards Lord Varahadev, you are slained! And then he smashed the Lord's club with his club and the Lord's club fell to the ground. And Lord Varahadev was without a weapon and Hiranyaksa was thinking, ha, ha, ha, ha, ha! just see my power. You think you can fight against me? And Lord Varahadev…the demigods they were thinking, oh my God! Vishnu is about to be slained. And Hiranyaksa was convinced that he had already shown that he was more powerful, now was just a matter of time till he could kill God. But Lord Varahadev, He was very confident, simply smiling at the whole situation.

That is the condition. This material world is God's energy. Sometimes Krishna through material existence, allows the illusioned conditioned soul to think that, yes, I am conquering the multifarious opponents in the form of the miseries of material existence. I do have healthy body and nice wealth and good intelligence. And I do have a very peaceful situation in life. I am conquering hunger and thirst and heat and cold and honour and dishonour. I am conquering these things. Just see what I have done with my own hands. Just see how responsible and pious I am. I am happy. I am winning the war. It's a hard struggle, but it's worth it. This is the mentality of Hiranyakasipu…Hiranyaksa and Hiranyakasipu. Whether you are pious or impious, in this world you are fighting against the miseries of material existence. And then in the end, the material [energy]…Maya is simply smiling at you [chuckles]. You think you are defeating me? [chuckles] I am just giving you a little time to play with you and you just wait, in due course of time what did Varahadev do to Hiranyaksa after this whole battle where Hiranyaksa was displaying all his mystic powers and through most of the battle it appeared that Varahadev was losing and Hiranyaksa was convinced he was going to win. He was creating such illusions with his mystic powers and then in the end, Varahdev thought he thinks he is winning. I let him go long enough in his illusion. And then he simply…Varahadev simply took his lotus like hmm…hoof…boars have hooves…and he just touched him behind the ear just went like this [Maharaja makes sound of soft tap]. And just by touching him…here they were beating each other with clubs and smashing each other with fists and weapons, it was a very terrible war. But in the end, he just took…just his little finger went and it was like a thunderbolt into Hiranyaksa’s head. And he just…he just fell to the ground dead instantly and the whole earth shook. That is the destiny of every materialistic person.

Instead of struggling for sense enjoyment, struggle for Krishna and that will take you to Vaikuntha

So rather than simply fight this hard struggle for material existence, we have to give up our insatiable material desires to the process of purification, through the process of associating with saintly persons, through the process of chanting the names of God, through the process especially of dovetailing our natural inclinations to struggle, to struggle for Krishna. So, this lady last night who was asking how can you expect me to be steady in devotional service, but it's so difficult. This world is a difficult place. This world is a place of a hard struggle for existence. Whether you are a devotee or whether you are a materialist in the mode of goodness, passion and ignorance, you have to struggle in this world. The question is, what are you going to struggle for and who are you going to struggle for? If you struggle for your own profit, adoration and distinction in this world, you have danger at every step, you will fall into the ocean today or tomorrow, suffer and die. And no one can deny that that is a fact. But if you struggle for Krishna, if you accept hardships and difficulties on behalf of Krishna, in the service of the Lord and His devotees, then you are transported beyond this ocean of material existence. And you realize that within your own heart is the spiritual world Vaikuntha, that place where there is no anxieties and you can live there eternally. Well, in this body, you can live in that place. You can be in this world, but not of this world. You can see it for what it is and see you in your real relationship with God. Krishna says in Bhagavad-gita

janma karma ca me divyam
evaṁ yo vetti tattvataḥ
tyaktvā dehaṁ punar janma
naiti mām eti so ’rjuna

[Bg. 4.9]

[One who knows the transcendental nature of My appearance and activities does not, upon leaving the body, take his birth again in this material world, but attains My eternal abode, O Arjuna.]

‘One who understands the transcendental nature of my activities and birth, one who engages in my loving service, he never takes birth in this material world again.’ This material world where from the highest, most pious condition of life to the most sinful hellish condition, they are all miserable, where there is danger at every step, where death is the conclusion. But one who attains my abode, Krishna says will never suffer again. In the spiritual world, there is no struggle, there is no hardships. Everyone is simply dancing and chanting. It is a joyful existence. But in this material world, there is only a struggle. No matter who you are, no matter what you do, today or tomorrow, your life is a struggle. And it's a struggle like the struggle of Hiranyakasipu against Varahadev, it's a struggle against an enemy that you can never defeat. It is a struggle against the almighty destiny of death. So therefore, this great Avadhuta is describing to Prahlad Maharaj, how he is taking the position of explaining the journey of the soul, in this material world. ‘Because of insatiable material desires, I was being carried away by the waves of material nature's laws. And thus, I was engaging in different activities struggling for existence in various forms of life.’

If we can simply release ourselves from the bondage of insatiable material desires, then we are freed from all the sufferings of material existence. It is our desires to enjoy this material world that is like a chain that keeps us bound to this condition of great suffering. So, we must purify our desires by constantly engaging our mind and senses, by good direction of saintly persons in the service of Lord Sri Hari. When our mind and senses are engaged in the service of Sri Sri Radha Gopinath, we should know we are no longer in this material world, but we are in route to the spiritual world Vaikuntha.

Thank you very much. Hare Krishna

Is there any question?

Q&A Transcription

[devotee:] [00:36:00-00:36:21 inaudible-question not clear]

Maharaj: They are so attached to their sense enjoyment. They cannot give it up, even though they know they will be destroyed. They are not willing to admit, even though they know that it's a fact. They are not willing to admit it. Because they do not want to give up their sense enjoyment. Ha! this is the power of Maya’s illusion. Try to understand. The power of this illusory energy is indescribable. Ha! Any other question?

[devotee:] [00:37:15-00:38:10 inaudible-question not clear]

[Maharaja:]Maharaj: The example you used that of a pig. Hmm. Obviously, this is a very well thought out example, because you did not say anything whimsically. Everything that Maharaj says is very, very systematically, methodically analysed before it’s spoken. So, he used the example of a pig. Our pig is thinking he is…at least he is trying for happiness. Ha, he is happy. So then, become a pig. Indra was also thinking like this. But how long does that pursuit of happiness, that illusion of happiness remains? That is the question, it cannot remain. It is described that Indra because of his pride, he committed offence to a great soul. So, he was cursed to become a pig. Try to understand, he is such an illustrious personality with so much responsibility, with so much physical beauty, with so much facility for opulence all around him and he was cast into such a degraded body as a pig. Of course, I don't know if you want to consider a pig, a degraded body. But most people would say, from India, to become a pig is a great state of degradation. And he was just…just like you are saying, he was enjoying the life of a pig. He had his little piggies. So many little children and he had a beautiful pig wife. Can you imagine, he gave up Urvashi and all these heavenly damsels, his wife Sachi for a female pig. And he was thinking she is so beautiful and nice and so kind and so she just makes me feel so good. And my children, they stay stay…they have such sweet little childish words [Maharaja makes sound of baby pigs]. To a pig that's sweet, to you it's disgusting. But for a pig, it's sweet. And I get to eat nice fresh stool every day. My wife prepares nice fresh stool everyday [Maharaja laughs] with great care. And in this way, he was enjoying like anything. And Lord Brahma, he saw that the heavens were in disorder because Indra was neglecting his service. So, he said okay Indra, the curse is finished, now come back to your heavenly planet. Indra said, forget it. I have a wife, I have children, I have a home, I have...I have a life. I have made a life for myself here. I am happy. Ha, just like what you are saying. So, Lord Brahma let him for a while go on, until one day, proprietor of the farm that pig was living, decided it's time to slaughter the pigs. So right in front of Indra, the farmer took first took all of his little piglets, his little children that were so dear to his life. And he took them by the feet. And he took a knife and cut off their heads. And Indra was thinking oh my God! [Maharaja makes sound of slaughter] And then…and then he took the wife and slaughtered her. And then he picked up Indra by the feet and took the knife. And just when he was about to slaughter him, Indra just cried out to Brahma, take me back, get me out of this horrible, miserable place. So, you see, as long as we have mentalities of pigs śva-viḍ-varāhoṣṭra-kharaiḥ saṁstutaḥ puruṣaḥ paśuḥ [SB 02.03.19], those who have mentality of pigs, hogs, they think they are happy in this world. They are struggling so hard, just for a little stool of material enjoyment and under the illusion, but they do not know that at any moment the sword of death is about to sever them from all the things that are near and dear to them. Thank you very much.
